places.name: Veranda Brasserie & Bar
places.near: U2/U3 Volkstheater ~2 min (Burggasse exit)
places.order: Veal schnitzel / beef tartare / asparagus soup / homemade ice cream
places.famousFor: Stylish hotel brasserie, Neubau; champagne bar (60+ labels)
places.goodToKnow: Booking advised / $$$ / opposite MuseumsQuartier
places.address: Hotel Sans Souci Wien, Burggasse 2, 1070 Wien

places.name: Shamshiry
places.near: Tehran: Panzdah-e-Khordad metro (L1) to Bazaar / Vienna VA: taxi/drive, Tysons
places.order: Chelo kabob barg / kabob kubideh / saffron basmati rice
places.famousFor: Legendary chelo kabab from Tehran's Grand Bazaar
places.goodToKnow: Reservations advised / $$ / Persian halal-style meat

places.name: Phoenicia Resto & Lounge
places.near: Dunn Loring (Orange) ~10 min taxi / Gallows Rd
places.order: Mixed mezze / grilled kebabs / shawarma / hummus
places.famousFor: Lebanese dining with live Middle Eastern music Fri/Sat
places.goodToKnow: Booking advised weekends / $$ / halal

places.name: Lexington Grill
places.near: No metro/tram — taxi/drive / Al Hamra
places.order: Dry-aged steak / Dibba Bay oysters / seafood
places.famousFor: NY-style dry-aged steakhouse, sea views
places.goodToKnow: Booking advised / $$$$ / serves alcohol
places.address: Vienna St - Al Jazeera Al Hamra-Qaryat Al Hamra - Ras Al Khaimah

places.name: Pastamara - Bar con Cucina
places.order: Spaghetti gambero e limone; vitello tonnato; swordfish with almond-pistachio crust
places.famousFor: Modern reinterpretation of Sicilian cuisine by 2-Michelin-star chef Ciccio Sultano; a stylish 'bar con cucina' off the Ritz-Carlton lobby
places.goodToKnow: In the Michelin Guide (not starred); evening six-course surprise menu plus a moderate two-course lunch; booking recommended. Schubertring 5-7

places.name: Chima Steakhouse
places.near: Tysons business district, near Tysons Corner mall
places.order: Picanha and assorted rodizio meats; salad bar
places.famousFor: Brazilian rodizio with gauchos carving continuous meats tableside; gourmet salad bar
places.goodToKnow: Upscale; all-you-can-eat rodizio; lunch & dinner; reservations recommended

places.name: Steirereck im Stadtpark
places.near: U4 Stadtpark, then a short walk into the Stadtpark; also tram 2 to Weihburggasse
places.order: Tasting menu; signature char 'cooked' in beeswax; heritage vegetables; the legendary cheese trolley and bread cart
places.notes: Luxury; Instagrammable (glass pavilion in the park). Michelin 2-star.
places.famousFor: Austria's most celebrated fine-dining table, holding two Michelin stars and long ranked among the World's 50 Best. Chef Heinz Reitbauer champions hyper-local, seasonal Austrian ingredients and forgotten vegetable varieties.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation essential (book weeks ahead). Typically closed weekends; verify 2026 hours. Smart-casual. The ground-floor Meierei is a more casual family-friendly alternative.

places.name: Amador
places.near: U4 to Heiligenstadt, then bus 38A toward Grinzing; taxi recommended for evenings
places.order: The multi-course grand tasting menu; bold flavour-contrast signatures built on seasonal luxury produce
places.famousFor: Austria's only three-Michelin-star restaurant, set in the vaulted wine cellars of a Grinzing estate. Chef Juan Amador fuses Spanish roots with cutting-edge technique.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation essential; tasting-menu only; expect a long, formal evening. Closed early in the week; verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Silvio Nickol Gourmet Restaurant
places.near: U3 Stubentor or U4 Stadtpark, a few minutes' walk to Coburgbastei
places.order: Multi-course degustation menu with optional wine pairings from the Palais Coburg cellar
places.famousFor: Two-Michelin-star restaurant inside the palatial Palais Coburg, renowned equally for its cuisine and one of the world's greatest wine cellars.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation essential; degustation-only; jacket advisable. Closed Sun–Mon typically; verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Konstantin Filippou
places.near: U1/U4 Schwedenplatz, ~6 min walk; or U3 Stubentor
places.order: Seasonal tasting menu; ask about the counter/chef's-table experience; adjacent wine bar 'O boufés' for a lighter visit
places.famousFor: Two-Michelin-star chef's-table cooking with a Greek-Austrian sensibility; refined, produce-driven and widely rated among Vienna's very best.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation essential; menu-only format. Closed weekends typically; verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Mraz & Sohn
places.near: U6 Jägerstraße or U4 Friedensbrücke, ~8–10 min walk
places.order: The surprise tasting menu (the kitchen leads; go with it)
places.famousFor: Two-Michelin-star, family-run and delightfully unpretentious — a residential-street address hiding some of Austria's most creative, boundary-pushing cooking.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation essential; tasting-menu only; small room. Closed weekends typically; verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Pramerl & the Wolf
places.near: U4 Roßauer Lände, ~7 min walk; near the Servitengasse quarter
places.order: The set surprise menu; creative takes on Austrian classics
places.famousFor: A tiny former corner-tavern turned one-Michelin-star darling, blending neighborhood warmth with inventive, produce-led tasting menus.
places.goodToKnow: Very small; reservation essential. Closed some weekdays; verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Figlmüller (Bäckerstraße)
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz, ~4 min walk; sister branch at Wollzeile 5 nearby
places.order: The oversized Wiener Schnitzel (traditionally pork); Figlmüller house salad; Apfelstrudel
places.famousFor: Vienna's legendary 'home of the Wiener Schnitzel' since 1905 — the plate-sized, pounded-thin schnitzel is an institution beloved by families and tourists alike.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily ~11:30–23:30 (Bäckerstraße). Reservation strongly advised; queues are long — go early. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Plachutta Wollzeile
places.near: U3 Stubentor or U1/U3 Stephansplatz, ~5–7 min walk
places.order: Tafelspitz served in its copper pot with the classic accompaniments (rösti, apple-horseradish, chive sauce); Wiener Schnitzel
places.famousFor: The definitive address for Tafelspitz (boiled beef in broth), where Viennese families gather for milestones. A polished, quintessentially Viennese dining experience.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation essential (1–2 weeks ahead). Open daily; verify 2026 hours. Portions are generous.

places.name: Plachuttas Gasthaus zur Oper
places.near: U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz or U3 Stephansplatz, a few minutes' walk to Walfischgasse
places.order: Wiener Schnitzel (crisp, golden veal); Tafelspitz; classic Viennese desserts
places.notes: Family-friendly; Viennese classic. Needs verification (confirm exact house number).
places.famousFor: The Plachutta family's cosy-yet-stylish house by the State Opera, prized for benchmark Wiener Schnitzel and classic Viennese fare.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation advised, especially pre/post-opera. Open daily; verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Meissl & Schadn (Grand Ferdinand)
places.near: Tram 2/71 or U-Bahn to Stadtpark/Karlsplatz; on Schubertring by the Ringstraße
places.order: Wiener Schnitzel vom Kalb (veal); Tafelspitz; Viennese classics with tableside flourish
places.famousFor: An elegant Ringstraße dining room in the Grand Ferdinand hotel famous for arguably the city's finest Wiener Schnitzel, prepared in an open show-kitchen.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation advised. Open daily; verify 2026 hours. Elegant but relaxed.

places.name: Zum Schwarzen Kameel
places.near: U3 Herrengasse, ~3 min walk; near Graben/Kohlmarkt
places.order: The famous open-faced canapé sandwiches (Brötchen) at the standing bar; Wiener Schnitzel; Tafelspitz
places.famousFor: A Vienna institution dating to 1618, blending a jewel-box Jugendstil deli/bar with an elegant restaurant; celebrated for its open-faced sandwiches and refined Viennese cooking.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation advised for the restaurant; the bar is walk-in for snacks. Verify 2026 hours and closing days.

places.name: Griechenbeisl
places.near: U1/U4 Schwedenplatz, ~3 min walk to Fleischmarkt
places.order: Wiener Schnitzel; Tafelspitz; goulash; classic Austrian fare
places.famousFor: One of Vienna's oldest inns (roots to the 15th century), a warren of historic vaulted rooms once frequented by Mozart, Beethoven and Mark Twain — signatures are carved into the famous guest room.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation advised. Open daily; verify 2026 hours. Very atmospheric, tourist-popular.

places.name: Gasthaus Pöschl
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz, ~5 min walk
places.order: Beuschel (Viennese ragout); Schnitzel; seasonal daily specials
places.notes: Hidden gem; family-friendly. Needs verification (confirm exact house number / website).
places.famousFor: A small, authentic Beisl just off pretty Franziskanerplatz, beloved by locals for honest, well-executed Viennese home cooking in a cosy setting.
places.goodToKnow: Very small; reservation strongly advised. Verify 2026 hours and closing days.

places.name: Glacis Beisl
places.near: U2/U3 Volkstheater or U2 MuseumsQuartier, ~4 min walk via Breite Gasse
places.order: Schnitzel; Austrian seasonal dishes; long lazy lunches in the garden
places.famousFor: A 'secret garden' Beisl tucked into the old city-wall grounds behind the MuseumsQuartier, famous for its leafy courtyard and modern take on Viennese bistro classics.
places.goodToKnow: Garden is seasonal (spring–autumn) and very popular; reserve for the terrace. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Gasthaus Ubl
places.near: U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz or U4 Kettenbrückengasse, ~6–8 min walk
places.order: Schnitzel; Tafelspitz; Bauernschmaus; seasonal Viennese daily dishes
places.notes: Hidden gem; family-friendly. Needs verification (no official website).
places.famousFor: A time-capsule, family-run Beisl near Naschmarkt that feels untouched by the decades — old-school charm, wood panelling and hearty classic Viennese cooking.
places.goodToKnow: Cash-friendly, phone reservations, no real website — part of its charm. Closed some days; verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Gasthaus Wolf
places.near: U4 Kettenbrückengasse or U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz, ~7 min walk
places.order: Schnitzel; goulash; seasonal Austrian specials
places.famousFor: A quintessential neighborhood Beisl a short stroll from Ubl, praised for excellent Viennese cooking and a welcoming bar-front atmosphere away from the crowds.
places.goodToKnow: Small; reservation advised. Verify 2026 hours and closing days.

places.name: Zum Holunderstrauch
places.near: U3 Zieglergasse, a few blocks' walk; near Neubaugasse
places.order: Schnitzel; hearty Viennese classics; daily specials
places.notes: Hidden gem; family-friendly. Needs verification (address/website not confirmed).
places.famousFor: An unassuming Neubau local tavern populated by regulars rather than tourists, serving straightforward, hearty Viennese comfort food at fair prices.
places.goodToKnow: Small neighborhood spot; hours limited — verify 2026 opening and exact address before visiting.
places.address: Zum Holunderstrauch, 3, Schreyvogelgasse, Schottenviertel, Katastralgemeinde Innere Stadt, Innere Stadt, Wien, 1010, Österreich

places.name: Stomach
places.near: U4 Roßauer Lände, ~8 min walk into the Servitenviertel
places.order: Whatever's on the seasonal weekly menu; hearty mains and homemade desserts
places.famousFor: A long-running (30+ years) off-the-beaten-path favourite with a charming courtyard garden and a weekly-changing menu built on quality Austrian produce.
places.goodToKnow: Limited opening days; garden seasonal; reservation advised. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Café Central
places.near: U3 Herrengasse, ~2 min walk
places.order: Viennese pastries and Central's own cakes; Wiener Schnitzel; Tafelspitz; a Melange coffee
places.famousFor: Vienna's most dramatically beautiful coffeehouse, opened 1876 in the neo-Gothic Palais Ferstel — the historic haunt of Freud, Zweig and Trotsky, with soaring arches and marble columns.
places.goodToKnow: Expect queues (no reservations for most seating); go early. Open daily; verify 2026 hours. Live piano some hours.

places.name: Café Landtmann
places.near: Tram 1/71/D to Rathausplatz/Burgtheater; U2 Rathaus, ~5 min walk
places.order: Tafelspitz; Wiener Schnitzel; the famous cake selection; Einspänner coffee
places.famousFor: Elegant Ringstraße coffeehouse since 1873 beside the Burgtheater; a favourite of politicians and actors, known as the best of the grand cafés for a full lunch.
places.goodToKnow: Reservations possible and advised for meals; large terrace. Open daily; verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Café Sperl
places.near: U2 MuseumsQuartier, ~5 min walk to Gumpendorfer Straße
places.order: Sperl-Torte; fresh strudel; a classic Melange; light Viennese lunch dishes
places.famousFor: A gloriously unmodernised 1880 coffeehouse — its Art Nouveau wood panelling and billiard tables are said to be the city's most authentically preserved café interior.
places.goodToKnow: Cash appreciated; classic 'no rush' coffeehouse etiquette. Closed some hours/Sundays seasonally; verify 2026.

places.name: Café Prückel
places.near: U3 Stubentor / tram 2 to Stubentor, right outside
places.order: Cakes and strudel from the counter; Melange; light Viennese dishes
places.famousFor: A listed 1950s-design coffeehouse (Oswald Haerdtl interior) opposite the MAK, cherished for its retro-nostalgic rooms and live piano evenings.
places.goodToKnow: Cash-friendly; piano some evenings. Open daily; verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Vollpension (Schleifmühlgasse)
places.near: U4 Kettenbrückengasse or U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz, ~6 min walk
places.order: Oma's homemade cakes and Buchteln; apple strudel; brunch; hot chocolate for kids
places.famousFor: An award-winning intergenerational social-enterprise café where grandmothers ('Omas') bake fresh cakes from family recipes in an open kitchen — cosy, quirky and hugely charming.
places.goodToKnow: Very popular for brunch — reserve or go early. Children's portions and high-chairs available. A second branch is at Johannesgasse 4A, 1010. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: NENI am Naschmarkt
places.near: U4 Kettenbrückengasse, right at the Naschmarkt
places.order: Hummus; sabich; the 'Balagan' mezze breakfast/spread; shakshuka; lamb kebab
places.famousFor: The buzzy market-side flagship of the NENI brand, serving vibrant, shareable Middle-Eastern-Mediterranean plates in an energetic Naschmarkt setting.
places.goodToKnow: Great for sharing/families; terrace popular. Reservation advised at peak times. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Motto am Fluss
places.near: U1/U4 Schwedenplatz, right at the station
places.order: Modern Austrian mains with organic focus; brunch; cakes in the upstairs café
places.famousFor: A striking 1950s-ocean-liner-styled café-restaurant built into the Danube-canal ferry terminal, with a coveted rooftop terrace over the water.
places.goodToKnow: Restaurant (lower level) reservation advised; café/terrace more casual. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Das LOFT Restaurant & Bar
places.near: U1/U4 Schwedenplatz, ~5 min walk across the canal; or U2 Taborstraße
places.order: Refined modern-European tasting/à-la-carte menu; the view is the headline
places.famousFor: An 18th-floor destination restaurant in the Jean-Nouvel-designed SO/ Vienna, celebrated for its dramatic multicolour glass ceiling and sweeping panoramic city views.
places.goodToKnow: Reservation essential; request a window table for the skyline. Smart dress. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Ristorante Settimo Cielo
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz, ~3 min walk
places.order: Classic Italian pasta and mains; a summer aperitivo on the terrace
places.notes: Instagrammable (cathedral view); family-friendly. Needs verification (confirm street/house number).
places.famousFor: A romantic 10th-floor Italian rooftop by St. Stephen's Cathedral, offering close-up cathedral views and sweeping panoramas over the historic centre.
places.goodToKnow: Terrace is best in warm months and small — reserve ahead. Verify 2026 hours and exact address.

places.name: Ludwig & Adele
places.near: U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz, ~3 min walk
places.order: Seasonal Austrian-international mains; strong vegetarian options; cakes and coffee
places.famousFor: A stylish, relaxed restaurant-café at the Künstlerhaus by Karlsplatz, a bit of a local hidden gem with a menu that's roughly half vegetarian and welcoming to families.
places.goodToKnow: Children's portions and high-chairs available. Reservation advised for dinner. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Ramasuri
places.near: U1 Nestroyplatz, ~2 min walk
places.order: Weekend brunch; porridge bowls; Mediterranean omelette; sharing plates
places.notes: Family-friendly; Instagrammable (casual-cool). Needs verification (confirm house number/website).
places.famousFor: A trendy all-day brunch and neighborhood spot on Praterstraße near the Nestroy monument, popular for relaxed daytime dining and strong veggie/vegan choices.
places.goodToKnow: Busy at weekend brunch — go early or reserve. Verify 2026 hours and exact house number.

places.name: Zanoni & Zanoni
places.near: A few steps from Stephansplatz / Stephansdom
places.order: Stracciatella or hazelnut from the 60+ flavours
places.famousFor: Vienna's best-known Italian ice-cream parlour, near Stephansplatz
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 7am-midnight, 365 days a year

places.name: Eissalon TICHY
places.near: At Reumannplatz, Favoriten (U1 Reumannplatz)
places.order: Eismarillenknödel (ice-cream apricot dumpling)
places.famousFor: Historic Viennese parlour (since 1952); home of the Eismarillenknödel
places.goodToKnow: Signature dumpling invented 1967; apricot ice cream wrapped in vanilla, rolled in nuts

places.name: Vienna State Opera
places.near: U-Bahn Karlsplitz station; central Vienna on the Ringstrasse
places.order: Grand arched facade lit at night; the sweeping staircase and loggia frescoes inside; framed from across the Ring
places.famousFor: Neo-Renaissance opera house on the Ringstrasse, one of the world's leading opera venues
places.goodToKnow: Exterior best at blue hour when floodlit; guided tours for the interior; busy tram/road in front

places.name: Wien Mitte The Mall
places.near: Landstrasser Hauptstrasse 1b, at Wien Mitte (U3/U4, S-Bahn, CAT airport train)
places.order: Fashion, shoes, beauty, electronics (Media Markt), books and stationery; Interspar supermarket
places.famousFor: Modern central Vienna mall above the Wien Mitte-Landstrasse transport hub
places.goodToKnow: ~60 shops; handy for the City Airport Train; closed Sundays

places.name: Café Landtmann
places.order: Melange, Apfelstrudel, the house Landtmann cakes from its own bakery
places.famousFor: Grand traditional Viennese coffeehouse (since 1873) next to the Burgtheater; longtime haunt of politicians, actors and writers (Freud, Mahler)
places.goodToKnow: Elegant, busy; reservations recommended, especially for the terrace on the Ring

places.name: Café Hawelka
places.order: Original Buchteln (sweet baked dumplings, served warm in the evening), Melange
places.famousFor: Iconic old-world café (since 1906) with dim, smoky bohemian interior; long a meeting place for artists and writers
places.goodToKnow: Cash preferred; cramped, atmospheric; Buchteln traditionally available from 10pm

places.name: Café Sacher Wien
places.order: Original Sacher-Torte with unsweetened whipped cream, Wiener Melange, hot chocolate
places.famousFor: Home of the Original Sacher-Torte inside Hotel Sacher, behind the State Opera
places.goodToKnow: Expect queues; dress is smart-casual; the torte is also sold boxed to take away

places.name: Demel
places.near: On Kohlmarkt, a short walk from the Hofburg
places.order: Anna Torte, Sachertorte (Demel version), candied violets, Annatorte and hot chocolate
places.famousFor: Imperial-era pastry shop and former k.u.k. court confectioner (since 1786); ornate interior and display kitchen
places.goodToKnow: Premium prices; watch confectioners at work; busy with tourists

places.name: Café Mozart
places.near: Beside the Albertina, behind the Vienna State Opera
places.order: Mozart Torte (pistachio), Einspänner, Melange, Apfelstrudel
places.famousFor: Traditional coffeehouse behind the State Opera (since 1794); featured in the film The Third Man
places.goodToKnow: Cakes supplied daily from Landtmann's bakery; central and tourist-heavy

places.name: Café Central
places.near: Corner of Herrengasse and Strauchgasse, near the Hofburg
places.order: Apfelstrudel, Sachertorte, Melange; pastries from the front cake counter
places.famousFor: Landmark coffeehouse in Palais Ferstel with vaulted arches; former haunt of Trotsky, Freud and other intellectuals
places.goodToKnow: Long queues common; live piano in the afternoons; reservations help

places.name: Fenster Café
places.order: Fensterccino (espresso in a chocolate-lined waffle cone), filter coffee, the cornettoccino
places.famousFor: Tiny third-wave specialty spot serving coffee through a small street window (Fenster = window); voted among Europe's best coffee shops
places.goodToKnow: Walk-up window with little seating; cash-friendly; quality specialty beans

places.name: Veranda Brasserie & Bar
places.near: U2/U3 Volkstheater ~2 min (Burggasse exit)
places.order: Veal schnitzel / beef tartare / asparagus soup / homemade ice cream
places.famousFor: Stylish hotel brasserie, Neubau; champagne bar (60+ labels)
places.goodToKnow: Booking advised / $$$ / opposite MuseumsQuartier
places.address: Hotel Sans Souci Wien, Burggasse 2, 1070 Wien

places.name: Shamshiry
places.near: Tehran: Panzdah-e-Khordad metro (L1) to Bazaar / Vienna VA: taxi/drive, Tysons
places.order: Chelo kabob barg / kabob kubideh / saffron basmati rice
places.famousFor: Legendary chelo kabab from Tehran's Grand Bazaar
places.goodToKnow: Reservations advised / $$ / Persian halal-style meat

places.name: Phoenicia Resto & Lounge
places.near: Dunn Loring (Orange) ~10 min taxi / Gallows Rd
places.order: Mixed mezze / grilled kebabs / shawarma / hummus
places.famousFor: Lebanese dining with live Middle Eastern music Fri/Sat
places.goodToKnow: Booking advised weekends / $$ / halal

places.name: Lexington Grill
places.near: No metro/tram — taxi/drive / Al Hamra
places.order: Dry-aged steak / Dibba Bay oysters / seafood
places.famousFor: NY-style dry-aged steakhouse, sea views
places.goodToKnow: Booking advised / $$$$ / serves alcohol
places.address: Vienna St - Al Jazeera Al Hamra-Qaryat Al Hamra - Ras Al Khaimah

places.name: Park Hyatt Vienna
places.near: Old-town Vienna near Graben/Stephansplatz; ~30 min from Vienna (VIE) airport
places.order: Arany Spa with vault pool, suites overlooking the historic center, The Bank Brasserie
places.famousFor: Grand former bank headquarters in Vienna's Goldenes Quartier; the pool sits in the old bank vault
places.goodToKnow: 5-star, $$$$$, Hyatt; landmark pool in a former bank vault

places.name: Palais Coburg
places.near: Central Vienna on the Ringstrasse near Stadtpark; ~30 min from Vienna (VIE)
places.order: Large two-story suites, six wine cellars with 60,000+ bottles, two-Michelin-star Silvio Nickol restaurant
places.famousFor: 19th-century Vienna palace (the 'asparagus fortress') with one of Europe's most renowned wine cellars
places.goodToKnow: 5-star, $$$$$, Leading Hotels of the World; all-suite palace, legendary wine collection

places.name: Ferienhaus am See - Marco Winter
places.near: Langseitenrotte, Wienerbruck; ~2 km from Lassingfall, ~4 km to Annaberg ski area
places.order: Best for a self-catered lakeside stay with swimming, SUP and fishing
places.famousFor: Lakefront holiday house on the Wienerbruck reservoir with sauna and whirlpool
places.goodToKnow: Holiday house, $$$, 4 bedrooms, sauna, hot tub, outdoor fireplace; rated 4.9

places.name: Pastamara - Bar con Cucina
places.order: Spaghetti gambero e limone; vitello tonnato; swordfish with almond-pistachio crust
places.famousFor: Modern reinterpretation of Sicilian cuisine by 2-Michelin-star chef Ciccio Sultano; a stylish 'bar con cucina' off the Ritz-Carlton lobby
places.goodToKnow: In the Michelin Guide (not starred); evening six-course surprise menu plus a moderate two-course lunch; booking recommended. Schubertring 5-7

places.name: Chima Steakhouse
places.near: Tysons business district, near Tysons Corner mall
places.order: Picanha and assorted rodizio meats; salad bar
places.famousFor: Brazilian rodizio with gauchos carving continuous meats tableside; gourmet salad bar
places.goodToKnow: Upscale; all-you-can-eat rodizio; lunch & dinner; reservations recommended

places.name: Das Wasserschloß
places.order: Picturesque moated castle architecture reflected in its surrounding water.
places.famousFor: A historic moated 'water castle' (Wasserschloss) in the Wiener Neustadt area of Lower Austria, surrounded by water rather than a natural waterfall.
places.goodToKnow: Note: this is a Wasserschloss (moated castle), not a natural waterfall. Best photographed in good weather with calm water for reflections.

places.name: Prater
places.near: Prater, Vienna; U-Bahn U1/U2 to Praterstern, right by the entrance
places.order: A ride on the 1897 Riesenrad Ferris wheel and a turn on the Praterturm swing
places.famousFor: Vienna's historic public funfair with the giant Wiener Riesenrad
places.goodToKnow: The park grounds are free to enter and open year-round; you pay individually per ride, with no overall gate fee

places.name: WIPARK AKH Parkgarage
places.order: Parking for AKH hospital visits, MedUni Vienna and the surrounding 9th/8th-district area
places.famousFor: Large WIPARK underground garage serving the AKH Vienna university hospital (Währinger Gürtel 18-20, 9th district); ~2,500+ spaces
places.goodToKnow: Paid, open 24h; approx EUR 3.50/h, daily ~EUR 35 (day card ~EUR 27)

places.name: Schönbrunn Palace & Gardens
places.near: U4 to Schönbrunn or Hietzing, then a short walk; trams 10/60 to Schönbrunn.
places.order: Grand Tour of the state apartments with audioguide; then wander the free Baroque gardens up to the Neptune Fountain, the maze, and the Privy Garden.
places.famousFor: The Habsburgs' 1,441-room imperial summer residence and Vienna's most-visited attraction, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Home of Maria Theresa and a young Emperor Franz Joseph.
places.goodToKnow: Palace open daily from 09:30 (closing 16:00–18:30 seasonally); timed entry — book online in advance as slots sell out. Gardens free, gates open ~06:30. Verify 2026 hours online.

places.name: Gloriette & Café Gloriette (Schönbrunn)
places.near: Within Schönbrunn Park; U4 Hietzing then uphill through the gardens.
places.order: Walk up (10–15 min) for the panorama of the palace and Vienna skyline; the ground-floor café serves imperial breakfast, or pay for the rooftop viewing terrace.
places.famousFor: The 1775 arcaded hilltop pavilion crowning the Schönbrunn gardens, built to celebrate Habsburg power — one of the best budget viewpoints in Vienna.
places.goodToKnow: Café open daily ~09:00–20:00; the paid roof terrace runs seasonally (approx. Apr–early Nov), last entry 30 min before close. Verify 2026 terrace dates.

places.name: Tiergarten Schönbrunn (Vienna Zoo)
places.near: U4 to Hietzing, ~10 min walk; main entrance near Hietzinger Tor.
places.order: See the giant pandas (returned May 2025), koalas, orangutans, elephants and Siberian tigers; visit the historic Baroque central pavilion.
places.famousFor: Founded in 1752, the world's oldest continuously operating zoo, set within the Schönbrunn palace park with over 500 species including giant pandas.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily from 09:00; closing varies by month (16:30 winter to 18:30 summer). Adults ~€27, kids 6–18 ~€15.50, under 6 free. Verify 2026.

places.name: Upper Belvedere (Klimt's 'The Kiss')
places.near: Tram D to Schloss Belvedere; or U1 Südtiroler Platz + short walk.
places.order: See 'The Kiss' (1st floor, Gallery 10), other Klimt golden-period works, plus Schiele and Kokoschka; the Marble Hall and ceiling frescoes.
places.famousFor: Baroque palace of Prince Eugene of Savoy, now a museum housing the world's largest Gustav Klimt collection — including 'The Kiss'.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 09:00–18:00; timed tickets often sell out — book ahead. Under-19s free. Best early or after 16:30 to avoid crowds. Verify 2026.

places.name: Belvedere Palace Gardens
places.near: Tram D to Schloss Belvedere; U1 Südtiroler Platz.
places.order: Photograph the Upper Belvedere mirrored in the reflection pond (spectacular at dusk); stroll the free hedged terraces between the two palaces.
places.famousFor: Terraced Baroque gardens by Dominique Girard linking the Upper and Lower Belvedere, with fountains, sculptures and a famous reflecting pond.
places.goodToKnow: Main garden free, open daily from early morning to dusk (opens 06:30 Apr–Oct / 07:00 Nov–Mar). Alpine Garden and Privy Garden are paid. Verify 2026.

places.name: Hofburg — Sisi Museum & Imperial Apartments
places.near: U3 Herrengasse; U1/U3 Stephansplatz + short walk; entrance at Michaelerkuppel.
places.order: Tour the Sisi Museum, Imperial Apartments and Silver Collection with the free audioguide (13 languages); combine with the Imperial Treasury nearby.
places.famousFor: The Habsburgs' vast winter palace; the Sisi Museum and Imperial Apartments reveal the life of Empress Elisabeth ('Sisi') and Emperor Franz Joseph.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 09:00–17:30, last admission 16:30. Sisi Ticket combo (with Schönbrunn + Furniture Museum) saves ~25%. Book ahead. Verify 2026.

places.name: Imperial Treasury (Kaiserliche Schatzkammer)
places.near: U3 Herrengasse; entrance at the Schweizerhof (Swiss Courtyard) inside the Hofburg.
places.order: See the 10th-century Imperial Crown, the Austrian imperial crown, one of the world's largest emeralds, and imperial regalia across 21 rooms.
places.famousFor: Over a thousand years of Habsburg secular and ecclesiastical treasures in the oldest part of the Hofburg, including the Imperial Crown of the Holy Roman Empire.
places.goodToKnow: Open Wed–Mon 09:00–17:30 (closed Tuesday). Under-19s free. Part of the KHM. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: St. Stephen's Cathedral (Stephansdom)
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz (directly beneath the cathedral).
places.order: Climb the 343 steps of the South Tower for city views, take the lift up the North Tower to the Pummerin bell, and visit the catacombs.
places.famousFor: Vienna's Gothic landmark cathedral with its zigzag-tiled roof, 136 m 'Steffl' south tower and the massive Pummerin bell.
places.goodToKnow: Nave open Mon–Sat ~09:00–11:30 & 13:00–16:30, Sun/holidays 13:00–16:30; tower/catacomb tickets separate. Hours shift for services. Verify 2026.

places.name: Kunsthistorisches Museum (KHM)
places.near: U2 Museumsquartier; U2/U3 Volkstheater; trams to Ring/Volkstheater.
places.order: See the world's largest Bruegel collection; have coffee under the cupola in the ornate café; view the Kunstkammer of imperial curiosities.
places.famousFor: One of the world's great art museums, built for the Habsburg collections — Bruegel, Vermeer, Rembrandt, Caravaggio and an Egyptian collection, under a lavish domed hall.
places.goodToKnow: Open Tue–Sun 10:00–18:00 (Thu to 21:00); also open Mondays Jun–Aug. Adults ~€22 online; under-19s free. Verify 2026.

places.name: Albertina
places.near: U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz; U3 Herrengasse; trams to Oper.
places.order: See Monet, Picasso, Klimt and Dürer; walk the opulent Habsburg State Rooms; enjoy the terrace over the Opera.
places.famousFor: A Habsburg palace on the Ring housing a world-class graphic collection (Dürer's 'Young Hare') and the Batliner collection of Monet-to-Picasso modernism.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 10:00–18:00 (Mon & Fri to 21:00). Book online. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Leopold Museum (MuseumsQuartier)
places.near: U2 Museumsquartier; U2/U3 Volkstheater.
places.order: See ~300 Schiele works plus Klimt and Kokoschka; explore the 'Vienna 1900. Birth of Modernism' permanent show.
places.famousFor: Home to the world's largest Egon Schiele collection and a key 'Vienna 1900' Modernism display, at the heart of the MuseumsQuartier.
places.goodToKnow: Open Wed–Mon 10:00–18:00, closed Tuesday (daily in Jul/Aug). Adults ~€19. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: MuseumsQuartier (MQ) Courtyards
places.near: U2 Museumsquartier; U3 Volkstheater, 3-min walk.
places.order: Lounge on the colourful Enzi seats (free), see the rotating MQ Art Box, and climb the MQ Libelle rooftop viewing platform above the Leopold Museum.
places.famousFor: One of the world's largest cultural districts — former imperial stables now home to museums, cafés and lively courtyards with the signature 'Enzi' loungers.
places.goodToKnow: Courtyards open around the clock and free; you pay only for individual museums. Enzi colour/layout changes seasonally. Verify 2026.

places.name: Naturhistorisches Museum (Natural History Museum)
places.near: U2/U3 Volkstheater; trams to Ring; opposite the KHM on Maria-Theresien-Platz.
places.order: See the Venus of Willendorf in the Venus Cabinet, the dinosaur hall, the giant meteorite collection, and the rooftop terrace tour.
places.famousFor: Grand imperial natural-history museum (twin of the KHM) famous for the 29,500-year-old Venus of Willendorf and vast dinosaur and mineral halls.
places.goodToKnow: Open Wed–Mon 09:00–18:00 (Wed to 20:00), closed Tuesday — confirm current days/times online for 2026.

places.name: Austrian National Library — State Hall (Prunksaal)
places.near: U3 Herrengasse; U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz; entrance on Josefsplatz.
places.order: Wander the towering book-lined hall, admire the ceiling frescoes and antique globes — a top Instagram spot inside the Hofburg complex.
places.famousFor: An 80 m-long Baroque library hall of 1723 with a frescoed dome and ~200,000 historic books — one of the most beautiful libraries in the world.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 09:00–18:00 (Thu to 21:00); Oct–May closed Mondays. Under-19s free. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Vienna State Opera (Wiener Staatsoper)
places.near: U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz; trams 1/2/71/D to Oper.
places.order: Take the 40-minute guided tour of the grand staircase, tea salon, Marble Hall and auditorium; or attend a performance (standing-room tickets are famously cheap).
places.famousFor: One of the world's leading opera houses, a neo-Renaissance landmark on the Ring with a nightly world-class program.
places.goodToKnow: Tours (~€7–13, free for children) sold in the ticket shop or 30 min before on site; box office Mon–Sat 10:00–18:00. Verify 2026 schedule.

places.name: Wiener Musikverein (Golden Hall)
places.near: U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz, short walk; near the Ring behind the Imperial Hotel.
places.order: Attend a classical concert in the gilded Golden Hall, or take a guided tour of the building.
places.famousFor: Home of the Vienna Philharmonic and its New Year's Concert; the 1870 Golden Hall (Goldener Saal) is regarded as one of the world's finest concert halls for its acoustics.
places.goodToKnow: Concert-dependent; buy from the official box office to avoid touts. Guided tours run on select days — book ahead. Verify 2026 program.

places.name: Spanish Riding School (Spanische Hofreitschule)
places.near: U3 Herrengasse; entrance/visitor center at Michaelerplatz.
places.order: Watch a gala or standard performance, or the cheaper Morning Exercise training session under the chandeliers.
places.famousFor: A 450-year-old institution of classical dressage with white Lipizzaner stallions, performing in the Baroque Winter Riding School inside the Hofburg.
places.goodToKnow: Performances 45–90 min; horses take a training break ~end June to early August. Book tickets in advance. Verify 2026 calendar.

places.name: Karlskirche (St. Charles's Church)
places.near: U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz, directly below the church.
places.order: Ride the glass panorama lift 32 m up to view Rottmayr's dome frescoes face-to-face — a Europe-unique experience.
places.famousFor: Vienna's finest Baroque church (1737) with a green dome, two Trajan-style columns and a reflecting pool on Karlsplatz.
places.goodToKnow: Open Mon–Sat 09:00–12:30 & 13:00–18:00, Sun 12:00–17:30; single ticket ~€8 covers the lift and treasury. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Peterskirche (St. Peter's Church)
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz; just off the Graben on Petersplatz.
places.order: Admire the 56.8 m dome frescoes and gold altars; catch a free lunchtime organ recital.
places.famousFor: A jewel-box Baroque church (1703–1733) tucked off the Graben, with an oval frescoed dome and gilded interior — free to enter.
places.goodToKnow: Open Mon–Fri ~08:00–19:00, weekends/holidays from 09:00; admission free. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Kirche am Steinhof (Otto Wagner Church)
places.near: Bus 48A to Psychiatrisches Zentrum, then walk uphill; or bus 47A/48A from Ottakring (U3).
places.order: See the gilded dome, Koloman Moser stained glass and functional Secessionist details; enjoy the hilltop setting and city view.
places.famousFor: Otto Wagner's 1907 Art Nouveau (Jugendstil) masterpiece with a golden dome, on the grounds of a former psychiatric hospital.
places.goodToKnow: Seasonal — typically open ~Mar–Oct on limited days (e.g. Tue–Fri afternoons, Sat/Sun); ~€5. Winter break. Strictly confirm 2026 dates before going.

places.name: Imperial Crypt (Kaisergruft / Kapuzinergruft)
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz; U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz; on Neuer Markt.
places.order: See the ornate imperial sarcophagi across 10 vaults and Sisi's flower-adorned tomb beside Franz Joseph.
places.famousFor: The Habsburg burial vault beneath the Capuchin church since 1633, holding 142 family members including 12 emperors — and Empress Sisi.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 10:00–18:00, last entry 17:30; closed 1–2 Nov. Adults ~€8–15 (sources vary). Verify 2026.

places.name: Vienna Central Cemetery (Zentralfriedhof)
places.near: U3 to Simmering, then tram 11 or 71 to 'Zentralfriedhof 2. Tor'.
places.order: Visit the composers' graves in Group 32A, the imposing main gate and church; the park-like grounds are a peaceful walk.
places.famousFor: Europe's second-largest cemetery (1874), the resting place of Beethoven, Schubert, Brahms, Strauss and other musical greats, with the domed Art Nouveau Karl-Borromäus Church.
places.goodToKnow: Free; open daily (hours vary by month, extended Thu evenings May–Aug). Vast — allow time or take the internal bus. Verify 2026.

places.name: Wiener Prater & Riesenrad (Giant Ferris Wheel)
places.near: U1/U2 Praterstern, right by the entrance.
places.order: Ride the ~65 m Riesenrad for city views; enjoy the amusement rides in the Wurstelprater, or cycle/stroll the green Prater.
places.famousFor: Vienna's historic public park and Wurstelprater funfair, crowned by the 1897 Giant Ferris Wheel — a symbol of the city (featured in 'The Third Man').
places.goodToKnow: Ferris wheel open year-round (roughly 09:00–23:45, seasonal). Individual funfair rides mostly operate ~Mar–Oct/late autumn and are pay-per-ride. Verify 2026 season.

places.name: Haus des Meeres (Aqua Terra Zoo)
places.near: U3 Neubaugasse; in Esterházypark.
places.order: See sharks, rays and the Amazon tropical house; watch feedings; go up to the rooftop terrace for skyline views.
places.famousFor: An 11-floor aquarium and zoo inside a WWII flak tower, with sharks, a tropical house, and a rooftop café/terrace over Vienna.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 09:00–20:00, last entry 19:30. Adults ~€23.50, kids 6–15 ~€9.80. Book online. Verify 2026.

places.name: Technisches Museum Wien (Science & Technology)
places.near: U4 Schönbrunn + short walk; tram 52/60 or bus 10A to the museum; near Schönbrunn.
places.order: Explore the interactive miniXplore and minTi kids' zones, the high-voltage show, locomotives and the model railway.
places.famousFor: Vienna's hands-on museum of science and technology — transport, energy, music machines and a huge model railway — one of the best rainy-day family spots.
places.goodToKnow: Open Mon–Fri 09:00–18:00, Sat/Sun/holidays 10:00–18:00 (daily 10:00–18:00 in Jul/Aug). Under-19s free. Verify 2026.

places.name: House of Music (Haus der Musik)
places.near: U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz; U1/U3 Stephansplatz; near the Opera.
places.order: 'Conduct' the Vienna Philharmonic on the virtual-conductor screen, explore the Sonotopia sound worlds, and the composer rooms.
places.famousFor: An interactive sound museum over four floors, celebrating the Vienna Philharmonic and the city's great composers with playful sonic experiments.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 10:00–22:00, last admission 21:00. Adults ~€19; family ticket ~€35. Verify 2026.

places.name: Time Travel Vienna
places.near: U3 Herrengasse; U1/U3 Stephansplatz; on Habsburgergasse.
places.order: Do the guided tour through nine themed sets; kids especially enjoy the special effects and virtual-reality segments.
places.famousFor: A ~60-minute immersive walk-through of Vienna's history — Habsburgs, composers, the plague, WWII — with 5D cinema, animatronics and VR.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 10:00–20:00; tours start every 20 min, audioguide in 10 languages. Verify 2026 hours/prices.

places.name: Kunst Haus Wien & Hundertwasserhaus
places.near: Tram 1 or O to Radetzkyplatz (museum); Hundertwasserhaus at Kegelgasse 36–38.
places.order: Tour the Kunst Haus Wien museum (uneven floors, forest of columns), then photograph the free-to-view Hundertwasserhaus façade a few blocks away.
places.famousFor: Friedensreich Hundertwasser's whimsical, wonky architecture — the museum holds the largest permanent collection of his work; the nearby Hundertwasserhaus is an iconic colourful residence.
places.goodToKnow: Museum open daily 10:00–18:00 (~€11). Hundertwasserhaus is a private residence — view the exterior only, free, 24/7. Verify 2026.

places.name: Danube Tower (Donauturm)
places.near: U1 Kaisermühlen–VIC or U1 Alte Donau + walk/bus; in the Donaupark.
places.order: Take the 35-second express lift to the open-air and glazed terraces for panoramic views; dine in the rotating restaurant.
places.famousFor: At 252 m, Austria's tallest structure, with a 150 m observation deck and revolving restaurant offering 360° views over Vienna and the Danube.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 10:00–22:00 (last ascent 21:15); adults ~€18. Weather-dependent. Verify 2026.

places.name: Kahlenberg Viewpoint
places.near: U4 to Heiligenstadt, then bus 38A up via Grinzing (~30 min).
places.order: Take in the panorama from the terrace, visit St. Joseph's Church, and (if open) climb the Stefaniewarte; walk down through the Grinzing vineyards.
places.famousFor: A 484 m hill in the Vienna Woods with the city's best-known panoramic terrace, St. Joseph's Church and the Stefaniewarte tower — plus a key site of the 1683 siege.
places.goodToKnow: Free viewpoint, accessible year-round; church/tower hours vary. Bring layers — it's cooler up top. Verify 2026 tower access.

places.name: Naschmarkt
places.near: U4 Kettenbrückengasse (flea-market end) or U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz.
places.order: Graze the market stalls for Austrian and Middle-Eastern specialties, spices and sweets; browse the Saturday flea market at the Kettenbrückengasse end.
places.famousFor: Vienna's most famous market — 120+ stalls of fresh produce, spices, cheese and international foods stretching along the Wienzeile, with a Saturday flea market.
places.goodToKnow: Stalls Mon–Fri ~06:00–21:00, Sat to 18:00; food market CLOSED Sundays (restaurants only). Flea market Saturdays ~06:30–15:00. Verify 2026.

places.name: Schönbrunn Culture & Christmas Market
places.near: U4 Schönbrunn; in the palace forecourt.
places.order: Browse handicrafts, roast chestnuts and festive food; enjoy family activities against the palace backdrop.
places.famousFor: A festive market of ~90 stalls in the forecourt of Schönbrunn Palace, one of Vienna's most atmospheric Advent (and New Year) markets.
places.goodToKnow: SEASONAL: approx. 6 Nov 2026 – 6 Jan 2027; daily ~10:00–21:00 (reduced 24–25 Dec). Confirm exact 2026 dates before travel.

places.name: Rathausplatz Christmas Market (Wiener Christkindlmarkt)
places.near: U2 Rathaus; trams 1/71/D to Rathausplatz.
places.order: Wander the illuminated stalls for gifts and treats, skate on the ice paths, and photograph the illuminated Rathaus.
places.famousFor: Vienna's largest and most famous Christmas market on the square before the neo-Gothic City Hall, with 150+ stalls, an ice-skating 'Eistraum' and a park of lights.
places.goodToKnow: SEASONAL: roughly 13 Nov – 26 Dec 2026 (ice-skating continues to ~6 Jan 2027); Sun–Thu 10:00–21:30, Fri/Sat to 22:00. Confirm 2026 dates.

places.name: Café Landtmann
places.order: Melange, Apfelstrudel, the house Landtmann cakes from its own bakery
places.famousFor: Grand traditional Viennese coffeehouse (since 1873) next to the Burgtheater; longtime haunt of politicians, actors and writers (Freud, Mahler)
places.goodToKnow: Elegant, busy; reservations recommended, especially for the terrace on the Ring

places.name: Café Hawelka
places.order: Original Buchteln (sweet baked dumplings, served warm in the evening), Melange
places.famousFor: Iconic old-world café (since 1906) with dim, smoky bohemian interior; long a meeting place for artists and writers
places.goodToKnow: Cash preferred; cramped, atmospheric; Buchteln traditionally available from 10pm

places.name: Café Sacher Wien
places.order: Original Sacher-Torte with unsweetened whipped cream, Wiener Melange, hot chocolate
places.famousFor: Home of the Original Sacher-Torte inside Hotel Sacher, behind the State Opera
places.goodToKnow: Expect queues; dress is smart-casual; the torte is also sold boxed to take away

places.name: Demel
places.near: On Kohlmarkt, a short walk from the Hofburg
places.order: Anna Torte, Sachertorte (Demel version), candied violets, Annatorte and hot chocolate
places.famousFor: Imperial-era pastry shop and former k.u.k. court confectioner (since 1786); ornate interior and display kitchen
places.goodToKnow: Premium prices; watch confectioners at work; busy with tourists

places.name: Café Mozart
places.near: Beside the Albertina, behind the Vienna State Opera
places.order: Mozart Torte (pistachio), Einspänner, Melange, Apfelstrudel
places.famousFor: Traditional coffeehouse behind the State Opera (since 1794); featured in the film The Third Man
places.goodToKnow: Cakes supplied daily from Landtmann's bakery; central and tourist-heavy

places.name: Café Central
places.near: Corner of Herrengasse and Strauchgasse, near the Hofburg
places.order: Apfelstrudel, Sachertorte, Melange; pastries from the front cake counter
places.famousFor: Landmark coffeehouse in Palais Ferstel with vaulted arches; former haunt of Trotsky, Freud and other intellectuals
places.goodToKnow: Long queues common; live piano in the afternoons; reservations help

places.name: Fenster Café
places.order: Fensterccino (espresso in a chocolate-lined waffle cone), filter coffee, the cornettoccino
places.famousFor: Tiny third-wave specialty spot serving coffee through a small street window (Fenster = window); voted among Europe's best coffee shops
places.goodToKnow: Walk-up window with little seating; cash-friendly; quality specialty beans

places.name: Demel (K.u.K. Hofzuckerbäcker)
places.near: U3 Herrengasse, 3-min walk; on the Kohlmarkt luxury street
places.order: Demel's Sachertorte, Anna-Torte, or a slice from the daily display; hot chocolate
places.famousFor: Former imperial court confectioner and Sacher's rival for the 'original' Sachertorte. A preserved Jugendstil confectionery with glass-fronted cases and an open kitchen where the 'Demelinerinnen' work behind glass.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily approx. 8:00–19:00; small museum upstairs. Busy midday. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Café Sperl
places.near: U2 Museumsquartier, 6-min walk; tram/bus on Gumpendorfer Strasse
places.order: Sperl-Torte with a Melange; play a round at the billiard tables
places.famousFor: Time-capsule 1880 coffee house where little has changed — Thonet chairs, billiard tables and a Ringstrasse-era interior. A frequent film location (used in 'Before Sunrise'-era Vienna nostalgia and other productions).
places.goodToKnow: Approx. Mon–Sat 7:00–22:00, Sun 10:00–20:00; historically cash-preferred — check card acceptance. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Café Museum
places.near: U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz, 2-min walk; near the Secession
places.order: Melange with Kaiserschmarrn or a slice of cake
places.famousFor: Opened 1899 with an interior linked to architect Adolf Loos; a classic artists' café once frequented by Klimt, Schiele and Kokoschka. Spacious and unpretentious.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily, roughly 8:00–24:00. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Café Prückel
places.near: Tram 2 to Stubentor; U3 Stubentor, 2-min walk
places.order: Melange with Prückel's cakes or a Palatschinken
places.famousFor: Beloved for its preserved 1950s interior (a rare non-19th-century Kaffeehaus aesthetic) opposite the MAK. Live piano several evenings a week.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily approx. 8:30–22:00; piano Mon/Wed/Fri evenings ~19:00–22:00. Cash-friendly. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Café Frauenhuber
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz, 4-min walk
places.order: Melange with Sachertorte or a Wiener breakfast
places.famousFor: Often cited as Vienna's oldest surviving coffee house (site serving since 1824); Mozart and Beethoven are said to have performed in the building.
places.goodToKnow: Approx. Mon–Sat 8:00–24:00, Sun/holidays 10:00–22:00. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Kleines Café
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz, 4-min walk
places.order: Melange or espresso; small snack plates
places.famousFor: A tiny, atmospheric café on a picturesque square, designed by architect Hermann Czech; famously featured in the film 'Before Sunrise'. Charming summer terrace on the square.
places.goodToKnow: Approx. daily 10:00–02:00; very small, often cash-only — bring cash. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Café Bräunerhof
places.near: U3 Herrengasse or U1/U3 Stephansplatz, ~4-min walk
places.order: Kleiner Brauner with an Apfelstrudel; read the free newspapers
places.notes: traditional Kaffeehaus / hidden gem / literary history
places.famousFor: A wonderfully unreconstructed, old-school Kaffeehaus that was the favourite of writer Thomas Bernhard; live classical music on weekend afternoons.
places.goodToKnow: Approx. Mon–Fri 8:00–21:00, Sat 8:00–19:00, Sun 10:00–19:00; cash-preferred. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Café Diglas
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz, 4-min walk
places.order: Large slice of Sachertorte or Cremeschnitte with a Melange
places.famousFor: Plush 1923 coffee house known for generously oversized cake slices and a classic red-banquette interior; a relaxed alternative to the Sacher/Demel queues.
places.goodToKnow: Approx. Mon–Fri 8:00–22:30, Sat/Sun/holidays 9:00–22:30. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Gerstner K.u.K. Hofzuckerbäcker
places.area: 1st District – Innere Stadt (Kärntner Strasse, opposite the Opera)
places.near: U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz or tram to Opera, 2-min walk
places.order: Petit fours, Gerstner Torte, or afternoon 'Time for Two' pastry tier
places.famousFor: Former imperial court confectioner (est. 1847) with an opulent multi-floor salon and terrace on Kärntner Strasse — grand, gilded, and highly Instagrammable.
places.goodToKnow: Café/salon open daily, roughly 10:00–23:00. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Kurkonditorei Oberlaa
places.area: 1st District – Innere Stadt (Neuer Markt) + other branches
places.near: Neuer Markt branch: U1/U3 Stephansplatz, 4-min walk
places.order: Oberlaa Truffle-Torte, Apfelstrudel or Kaiserschmarrn with a Melange
places.famousFor: Beloved Viennese confectionery chain (since 1974) prized by locals for consistently excellent, less-touristy cakes; large, comfortable, family-friendly cafés.
places.goodToKnow: Central branches open daily approx. 8:00–20:00; multiple locations (Neuer Markt, Alser Strasse 31, Kärntner Strasse, spa Oberlaa). Verify 2026 hours per branch.

places.name: Aida
places.area: Multiple – flagship on Wollzeile / Stephansplatz (1st District)
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz for the central branches
places.order: Aida's Sachertorte or Cremeschnitte with a small Brauner
places.notes: family-friendly / Instagrammable / budget / Needs verification (branch-specific address)
places.famousFor: Vienna's classic pink-hued espresso-and-cake chain (since 1913) with retro 1950s interiors — an affordable, cheerfully Instagrammable spot for everyday Viennese pastries.
places.goodToKnow: Numerous city-wide branches, generally open daily from ~7:00/8:00; hours and exact addresses vary by location — confirm the specific branch for 2026.

places.name: Kaffemik
places.near: U3 Neubaugasse, 4-min walk
places.order: Filter/pour-over of the current guest roaster; flat white
places.famousFor: Pioneering minimalist third-wave bar in trendy Neubau, known for a rotating line-up of top European guest roasters and now its own roasting; a cornerstone of Vienna's specialty scene.
places.goodToKnow: Small space; hours vary (often closed one weekday) — check ahead. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Jonas Reindl Coffee Roasters
places.near: U2 Schottentor + many trams (1/71/D/37/38…), at the doorstep
places.order: House single-origin filter or a cortado; buy retail beans
places.famousFor: One of Vienna's flagship specialty roasters (roasting since 2014), aiming for full bean-to-cup transparency; the Schottentor café sits by the transit hub nicknamed 'Jonas Reindl'.
places.goodToKnow: Approx. Mon–Fri 8:00–17:30, Sat 9:30–17:30, Sun closed; roastery at Westbahnstrasse 13; multiple branches. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: POC – People on Caffeine
places.near: Tram 43/44 to Lange Gasse; U2 Rathaus ~8-min walk
places.order: Espresso-based drinks and filter; a slice of homemade cake
places.famousFor: Intimate new-wave coffee shop across from the University of Vienna's Alser Strasse campus; a favourite student-and-aficionado spot serving excellent artisan coffee and cake.
places.goodToKnow: Small; limited seating; hours vary (often closed Sundays) — check ahead. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Balthasar Kaffee Bar
places.near: U1 Nestroyplatz, 2-min walk
places.order: Flat white or cortado; single-origin filter
places.famousFor: Stylish 2014 coffee bar on Praterstrasse opened by Tyrolean ex-chef Otto Bayer; smooth, sustainably sourced espresso and a training ground for many of Vienna's baristas.
places.goodToKnow: Approx. Mon–Fri 7:30–19:00, Sat 9:00–17:00, Sun closed. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: CoffeePirates
places.near: Tram 5/33/37/38 near Spitalgasse/Universitätsstrasse; U6 Alser Strasse ~8-min walk
places.order: Organic single-origin filter; cappuccino
places.famousFor: Vienna's first certified-organic specialty roaster; a laptop-friendly café that travels to visit its growers twice a year, with all coffees certified organic and directly traded.
places.goodToKnow: Roaster + café; confirm current café address and hours via their website, as details should be verified for 2026.

places.name: Kaffeefabrik
places.near: U1 Taubstummengasse, 3-min walk
places.order: Espresso or filter of a house/direct-trade coffee; retail beans to go
places.famousFor: One of Vienna's first third-wave roastery-cafés (opened 2011), run by Tobias Radinger; organic-certified, direct-trade beans and a compact, bean-forward counter.
places.goodToKnow: Small café; limited seating; hours vary — check ahead. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Wolfgang Coffee
places.near: U3 Zieglergasse, 3-min walk
places.order: Espresso or flat white; take-away is the norm
places.famousFor: A design-led micro-café inside the Qwstion concept store, opened by a former Balthasar barista; espresso pulled on a classic Faema E71 using beans from local roaster Süssmund.
places.goodToKnow: Closed Sundays and bank holidays; small, mostly to-go. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Süssmund Kaffee (Roastery Showroom)
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz, 5-min walk
places.order: Filter of a current single origin; buy freshly roasted beans
places.famousFor: Well-regarded Vienna roaster (roasting in Simmering) supplying many of the city's specialty bars; its central showroom/coffee shop serves and sells its Specialty-grade beans.
places.goodToKnow: Shop approx. Mon–Fri 9:00–18:30, Sat 9:00–18:00; closed Sun/holidays. Roastery has no regular hours (call ahead). Verify 2026.

places.name: Kaffeemodul
places.near: Tram 2 to Josefstädter Strasse; U6 Josefstädter Strasse ~7-min walk
places.order: Espresso, cortado or a Nomi single-origin filter
places.famousFor: A celebrated 'hole-in-the-wall' specialty bar using carefully selected beans from local roaster Nomi; frequently praised as serving some of the best coffee in Vienna.
places.goodToKnow: Very small; approx. Mon–Fri 7:30–17:30, Sat 10:00–14:00, Sun closed. Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Alt Wien Kaffee
places.near: U1/U2/U4 Karlsplatz, 6-min walk; near Naschmarkt
places.order: Taste a fresh roast at the bar; buy Arabica/organic-fairtrade blends to take home
places.notes: specialty-coffee / roastery / hidden gem / Needs verification (exact street number)
places.famousFor: Long-running Vienna roaster (since 2000) roasting daily in small 12kg batches to produce 40+ varieties; a fragrant shop with a tasting bar near the Naschmarkt.
places.goodToKnow: Primarily a roastery/shop with tasting bar; confirm exact street number and shop hours for 2026.

places.name: CaffèCouture
places.near: U3 Herrengasse, 3-min walk
places.order: Espresso or flat white on the house roast; single-origin filter
places.notes: specialty-coffee / hidden gem / Needs verification (current address)
places.famousFor: Long praised as one of Vienna's original third-wave leaders (roasting its own 'Mariahilfer Melange' and single origins); a small, quality-obsessed espresso bar.
places.goodToKnow: Small; has operated from the Freyung/Palais Ferstel passage and other spots over the years — confirm current address and hours for 2026.

places.name: Konditorei L. Heiner
places.near: U1/U3 Stephansplatz, 4-min walk
places.order: Sachertorte, Cremeschnitte or petit fours with a Melange
places.famousFor: Historic former imperial-court confectioner (K.u.K. Hofzuckerbäcker) with a charming old-world upstairs café; the Wollzeile branch is a local favourite and less touristy than Kärntner Strasse.
places.goodToKnow: Café/shop open daily; two central branches (Wollzeile and Kärntner Strasse 21-23). Verify 2026 hours.

places.name: Café Hofburg (Hofzuckerbäckerei)
places.area: 1st District – Innere Stadt (inside the Hofburg palace)
places.near: U3 Herrengasse, 5-min walk; inside the Hofburg (Reichskanzleitrakt)
places.order: Kaisermelange and Sachertorte or Apfelstrudel
places.notes: luxury / Instagrammable / family-friendly / Needs verification (exact address & hours)
places.famousFor: A grand café set within the imperial Hofburg palace complex, offering an opulent 'palace café' setting and full Viennese cake selection just steps from the Sisi Museum.
places.goodToKnow: Located inside the palace; open daily with tourist-oriented hours; confirm exact entrance, address and 2026 hours before visiting.
